title: [ImportVerilog][MooreToCore][Sim] Support queue element/range extractions in ImportVerilog and Sim
body:
This PR adds support for queue range selections, and for converting `DynQueueExtractOp` to Sim.
The changes are listed below:
- Separate range selections in `Expressions.cpp` into `handleQueueRangeSelectExpressions` and `handleArrayRangeSelectExpressions`, since queue range selects can have dynamic lower and upper bounds.
- Add `upperIdx` to `DynQueueExtractOp`. Because this op returns a new queue, and queue size is dynamic, i.e. not a type parameter, we cannot infer this index, and it needs to be passed explicitly.
-  Add `QueueGetOp` and `QueueSliceOp` to Sim, matching with `ArrayGetOp` and `ArraySliceOp` in HW.

- `DynQueueExtractRef` op has also had its purpose slightly changed: As far as I can tell, the behavior of an lvalue range selection into a queue isn't set out in the spec (does it return a new queue reference whose elements, when written, write the original queue?). I also can't find any sample code/examples online. Therefore I assume for now that this behavior is not supported.

`DynQueueExtractRefOp`  will be implemented in a later PR: it involves touching llhd, which I haven't got around to just yet!
